subject: Business Studies
question_en: This Act requires businesses to display information of their products on packaging: A) Employment Equity Act (EEA), 1998 B) Labour Relations Act (LRA), 1995 C) Consumer Protection Act (CPA), 2008 D) National Credit Act (NCA), 2005. Choose the correct letter.
question_af: Hierdie Wet vereis dat besighede inligting van hulle produkte op verpakking vertoon: A) Wet op Gelyke Indiensneming (WGI), 1998 B) Wet op Arbeidsverhoudinge (WAV), 1995 C) Wet op Verbruikersbeskerming (WVB), 2008 D) Nasionale Kredietwet (NKW), 2005. Kies die korrekte letter.
memo_answer: C

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Smith Stores uses outdated computers which prevent access to online transactions. This challenge is classified as a ... factor of the PESTLE analysis. A) technological B) political C) legal D) social. Choose correct letter.
question_af: Smith Handelaars gebruik verouderde rekenaars wat toegang tot aanlyn transaksies verhinder. Hierdie uitdaging word as n ... faktor van die PESTWO-analise geklassifiseer. A) tegnologiese B) politiese C) wetlike D) sosiale. Kies die korrekte letter.
memo_answer: A

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Highway Manufacturing operate in the ... sector as they produce office chairs from timber and steel. A) tertiary B) primary C) economic D) secondary. Choose correct letter.
question_af: Hoofweg Vervaardiging is werksaam in die ... sektor aangesien hulle kantoorstoele uit hout en staal vervaardig. A) tersiere B) primere C) ekonomiese D) sekondere. Kies die korrekte letter.
memo_answer: D

subject: Business Studies
question_en: The role of the interviewer during an interview is to: A) make the interviewee feel at ease B) ask clarity-seeking questions about the position C) assist candidates in responding to questions D) be inquisitive and show interest in the business. Choose correct letter.
question_af: Die rol van die onderhoudvoerder tydens n onderhoud is om: A) die aansoeker gemaklik te laat voel B) vrae te vra om duidelikheid oor die posisie te verkry C) kandidate by te staan met die beantwoording van vrae D) ondersoekend te wees en belangstelling in die besigheid te toon. Kies die korrekte letter.
memo_answer: A
memo_steps: make the interviewee feel at ease
die aansoeker gemaklik te laat voel

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Top Limited implemented the ... step of the PDCA model by monitoring processes to determine whether they are functioning effectively. A) do B) check C) plan D) act. Choose correct letter.
question_af: Top Beperk het die ...-stap van die BDKH-model geimplementeer deur die monitering van prosesse om te bepaal of hulle effektief funksioneer. A) doen B) kontroleer C) beplan D) handel. Kies die korrekte letter.
memo_answer: B

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Complete the statement: The ... Development Strategy aims to increase access to programmes that train people. (Word bank: selection; job specification; National Skills; management; threat; placement; weakness; performance; job description; Human Resources)
question_af: Voltooi die stelling: Die ...-ontwikkelingstrategie beoog om toegang tot programme wat mense oplei, te verhoog. (Woordelys: keuring; posspesifikasie; Nasionale Vaardigheid; bestuur; bedreiging; plasing; swak punt; prestasie; posbeskrywing; Menslikehulpbron)
memo_answer: National Skills

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Complete: Eezy Electronics are losing customers due to the high crime rate in the area. This is classified as a ... in the SWOT analysis.
question_af: Voltooi: Eezy Elektronika verloor kliente as gevolg van die hoe misdaadkoers in die omgewing. Dit word as n ... in die SSGB-analise geklassifiseer.
memo_answer: threat

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Complete: The component of the job analysis that outlines the duties and responsibilities of the position is known as ...
question_af: Voltooi: Die komponent van die posontleding wat die pligte en verantwoordelikhede van die posisie uiteensit, staan as ... bekend.
memo_answer: job description

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Complete: Themba, the human resources manager, conducts background checks of applicants who qualify for the job during the ... procedure.
question_af: Voltooi: Themba, die menslikehulpbronbestuurder, voer gedurende die ...-prosedure agtergrondkontroles uit van die aansoekers wat vir die pos kwalifiseer.
memo_answer: selection

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Complete: Blue Manufacturer developed new methods and techniques to improve the quality of their products. This is known as quality ...
question_af: Voltooi: Blou Vervaardiger het nuwe metodes en tegnieke ontwikkel om die gehalte van hulle produkte te verbeter. Dit staan as gehalte-... bekend.
memo_answer: management

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Name any FOUR types of leave provisions as stipulated in the Basic Conditions of Employment Act (BCEA), 1997 (Act 75 of 1997).
question_af: Noem enige VIER tipes verlofbepalings soos in die Wet op Basiese Diensvoorwaardes (WBDV), 1997 (Wet 75 van 1997) voorgeskryf.
memo_answer: Annual leave; Sick leave; Maternity leave; Parental/Adoption/Paternity leave; Family responsibility leave. Any 4.
notes: Mark first FOUR only. 1 mark each.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Outline the role of SETAs in supporting the Skills Development Act (SDA), 1998 (Act 97 of 1998).
question_af: Beskryf kortliks die rol van SOOOs in die ondersteuning van die Wet op Vaardigheidsontwikkeling (WVO), 1998 (Wet 97 van 1998).
memo_answer: SETAs: Report to Director General; Promote and establish learnerships; Collect levies and pay grants; Provide accreditation for skills development facilitators; Register learnership agreements; Approve workplace skills plans; Monitor training by service providers; Allocate grants; Oversee training in different sectors; Develop sector skills plans.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: SCENARIO: Bona Frames (BF) are well-known for their quality sunglasses and reading spectacles. BF sales have decreased as Bright Eyewear sells unique sunglasses at reasonable prices. QUESTION: Identify the force of Porters Five Forces model applicable to BF. Motivate your answer by quoting from the scenario.
question_af: SCENARIO: Bona Rame (BR) is bekend vir hulle gehalte-sonbrille en -leesbrille. BR se verkope het gedaal aangesien Helder Oogprodukte unieke sonbrille teen billike pryse verkoop. VRAAG: Identifiseer die krag van Porter se Vyf Kragte-model wat op BR van toepassing is. Motiveer jou antwoord deur uit die scenario aan te haal.
memo_answer: Competitive rivalry/Power of competitors. Motivation: BF sales have decreased as Bright Eyewear sells unique sunglasses at reasonable prices.
memo_steps: Motivation from scenario quoting sales decrease
notes: 2 marks force, 1 mark motivation from scenario.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Explain the steps in strategy evaluation.
question_af: Verduidelik die stappe in strategie-evaluering.
memo_answer: Steps: Examine underlying basis of strategy; Look forward and backwards in implementation; Compare expected vs actual performance; Determine reasons for deviations; Take corrective action; Set specific dates for control and follow up; Draw up table of advantages/disadvantages; Decide on desired outcome; Consider impact in internal and external environments.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Discuss any TWO types of intensive strategies.
question_af: Bespreek enige TWEE tipes intensiewe strategiee.
memo_answer: Market penetration: Products penetrate existing market at low price until well known; Focus on existing products in existing markets to increase market share; Aggressive marketing. Market development: Sell existing products in new markets; Target consumers outside normal target market; Restructure prices for all income levels. Product development: Introduce new products into existing markets; Generate new ideas for existing markets; Conduct test marketing. Mark first TWO only.
notes: Mark first TWO only. 2 marks name + 1 mark description. Submax 3 each.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Recommend other ways in which businesses can comply with the National Credit Act (NCA).
question_af: Beveel ander maniere aan waarop besighede aan die Nasionale Kredietwet (NKW) kan voldoen.
memo_answer: Comply with NCA: Offer pre-agreement statements; Disclose all costs/no hidden costs; Register with National Credit Regulator; Submit annual compliance report; Conduct affordability assessment; Verify identity of clients and report suspicious transactions/train staff on FICA.
memo_steps: Disclose all costs no hidden costs
notes: Max 4 marks. Do not award for responses from scenario in 2.6.1.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Explain ways in which businesses can apply ownership as a pillar of the Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment Act (BBBEE), 2003 (Act 53 of 2003).
question_af: Verduidelik maniere waarop besighede eienaarskap as n pilaar van die Wet op Breebasis-Swart Ekonomiese Bemagtiging (BBSEB), 2003 (Wet 53 van 2003) kan toepas.
memo_answer: BBBEE Ownership: Include black people in shareholding/partnerships/franchises; Encourage small black investors to invest in big companies; EMEs with 50%+ black ownership promoted to level 3 of BEE scorecard; More opportunities for black people to become owners/entrepreneurs; Large businesses form joint ventures with small black-owned businesses; Businesses find it difficult to locate suitable black business partners; Many black people cannot afford shares.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Advise businesses on the advantages of diversification strategies.
question_af: Gee besighede raad oor die voordele van diversifikasiestrategie.
memo_answer: Diversification advantages: Increases sales/revenue/income and growth; Improves business brand/image; Reduces risk of relying on one product; Gain more technological capabilities; Sustained profitability from different product lines; Produce more output using less inputs; Retain competitive advantage; Remain relevant in dynamic environment; Establish additional markets.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Give any FOUR examples of fringe benefits.
question_af: Noem enige VIER voorbeelde van byvoordele.
memo_answer: Fringe benefits (any 4): Pension fund; Medical aid/Health Insurance; Funeral benefit; Provident fund; Allowances (car/travel/housing/cell phone/clothing); Performance based incentives; Bonus shares; Staff discount/Free meals/Canteen.
notes: Mark first FOUR only. 1 mark each.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: SCENARIO: Fabric Wholesalers (FW) sell different types of material. FW pay their employees according to the number of rolls of material they sell. QUESTION: Name the salary determination method applied by FW.
question_af: SCENARIO: Materiaal Groothandelaars (MG) verkoop verskillende tipes materiaal. MG betaal hulle werknemers volgens die aantal rolle materiaal wat hulle verkoop. VRAAG: Noem die salarisbepalingsmetode wat deur MG toegepas word.
memo_answer: Piecemeal/Stukwerk

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Explain the implications of the Labour Relations Act (LRA) on the human resources function.
question_af: Verduidelik die implikasies van die Wet op Arbeidsverhoudinge (WAV) op die menslikehulpbronfunksie.
memo_answer: LRA implications: Promotes resolution of labour disputes; Protects rights of employees/employers as in Constitution; Advances economic development and social justice; Provides for unresolved disputes referred to Labour Courts; Promotes orderly negotiations and employee participation; Workers cannot be easily dismissed as CCMA processes needed; Framework for bilateral meetings between employees trade unions and employers; HR manager should allow workers to form trade unions/collective bargaining; Clarify transfer of employment contracts.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Advise businesses on the reasons for the termination of an employment contract.
question_af: Gee besighede raad oor die redes vir die beeindiging van n indiensnemingskontrak.
memo_answer: Termination reasons: Dismissal for unsatisfactory performance/misconduct; No more work for redundant employees/restructuring; Retrenchment due to insolvency; Voluntary resignation for better opportunities; Retirement age reached; Incapacity due to illness/injuries; Mutual agreement; Contract expires.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Outline the difference between quality control and quality assurance.
question_af: Beskryf kortliks die verskil tussen gehaltebeheer en gehalteversekering.
memo_answer: Quality Control: Inspects final product; Checks during and after production; Ensures finished products meet standards; Setting targets and corrective measures. Quality Assurance: Standards met at every stage of process; Prevents mistakes from happening; Getting product right first time; Building in quality rather than checking quality.
memo_steps: QA prevent mistakes right first time
QA building in quality not checking
notes: Max 4 marks. Submax 2 each. Award max 2 if difference not clear.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Discuss the impact of total quality management (TQM) if poorly implemented by businesses.
question_af: Bespreek die impak van totale gehaltebestuur (TGB) indien besighede dit swak implementeer.
memo_answer: Poor TQM impact: Unrealistic deadlines not achieved; Employees inadequately trained resulting in poor quality products; Decline in productivity due to stoppages; Cannot make necessary changes to satisfy customers; Business reputation/image suffers due to poor quality; Customers choose alternatives; Investors withdraw if profits decline; Decline in sales as goods returned; High staff turnover due to poor skills development.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Describe the advantages of monitoring and evaluation of quality processes as a total quality management (TQM) element for large businesses.
question_af: Beskryf die voordele van monitering en evaluering van gehalteprosesse as n totale gehaltebestuur (TGB)-element vir groot besighede.
memo_answer: Advantages: Prevents product defects and minimises wastage; Minimise equipment breakdown; Get things right first time; Improve performance and maintain high quality standards; Improve current and future management of quality outputs; Provide clear indication about quality aspects contributing to goals; Modify interventions to improve efficient use of resources; Support management for informed decisions; Reduce production costs; Revise strategies to improve quality; Benchmarking for best practices; Quality circles evaluate progress.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Advise businesses on the quality indicators of the public relations function.
question_af: Gee besighede raad oor die gehalte-aanwysers van die openbarebetrekkingefunksie.
memo_answer: Quality indicators of PR: Dealing quickly with negative publicity; Providing regular positive press releases; Implement sustainable CSI programmes; Positive results of feedback from public surveys on business image; High standard of internal publicity/appearance of buildings/professional telephone etiquette; Deliver quality goods/services that promote brand/image with key stakeholders; Compliance with recent legislation such as BEE.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Name any TWO types of defensive strategies.
question_af: Noem enige TWEE tipes defensiewe strategiee.
memo_answer: Any 2 of: Divestiture/Ontbondeling; Retrenchment/Afleggings; Liquidation/Likwidasie
notes: Mark first TWO only. 1 mark each.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Outline the strategic management process.
question_af: Beskryf kortliks die strategiese bestuursproses.
memo_answer: Strategic management process: Clear vision/mission/measurable objectives; Identify opportunities/weaknesses/strengths/threats through environmental scanning; Tools: SWOT/Porter Five Forces/PESTLE; Formulate alternative strategies; Develop action plans with tasks/deadlines/resources; Implement strategies by communicating to stakeholders/organising resources/motivating staff; Continuously evaluate/monitor/measure strategies and take corrective action.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Discuss the impact of the Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Amendment Act (COIDA), 1997 (Act 61 of 1997) on businesses.
question_af: Bespreek die impak van die Wysigingswet op Vergoeding vir Beroepsbeserings en -siektes (WVBBS), 1997 (Wet 61 van 1997) op besighede.
memo_answer: COIDA Positives: Promotes safety in workplace; Domestic workers can claim compensation; Employees do not contribute to fund; Simple claiming processes; Eliminates time and costs on civil court proceedings; Compensation exempt from tax; Employers protected from financial burden; More socially responsible; Workers treated with dignity. COIDA Negatives: Claiming processes time consuming; Military workers not covered; Foreign country workers not covered; Heavy penalties for negligence; Expensive implementation.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Recommend ways in which businesses can deal with the challenges posed by the social factor of the PESTLE analysis.
question_af: Beveel maniere aan waarop besighede die uitdagings wat deur die sosiale faktor van die PESTWO-analise gestel word, kan hanteer.
memo_answer: Dealing with social challenges: Sell substitute products at lower prices/Employ local community people; Learn local/indigenous languages/Hire employees who know local language; Work with community police forums for security; Know demands and trends of customers; Develop/produce/innovate new products addressing lifestyle trends.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Outline the purpose of induction.
question_af: Beskryf kortliks die doel van induksie.
memo_answer: Purpose of induction: Introduce new employees to management/colleagues; Tour of building/office; Make new employees feel welcome; Improve skills through in-service training; Familiarise with organisational structure/supervisors; Allow questions to reduce anxiety; Experience different departments; Explain safety regulations; Ensure employees understand roles/responsibilities; Communicate products/services information; Communicate business policies on ethical conduct.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Explain the Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) as a benefit required by law.
question_af: Verduidelik die Werkloosheidsversekeringsfonds (WVF) as n voordeel wat deur die Wet vereis word.
memo_answer: UIF: Employees contribute 1% of basic salary; Employers contribute 1% of employee basic salary; Employers pay 2% total directly to UIF per month; Fund assists dependants of deceased contributing worker; Short-term financial assistance when unemployed or unable to work due to illness/maternity/adoption leave; Contributions paid to UIF/SARS.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Explain the benefits of a good quality management system.
question_af: Verduidelik die voordele van n goeie gehaltebestuurstelsel.
memo_answer: Good QMS benefits: Effective customer services resulting in increased satisfaction; Time and resources used efficiently; Productivity increases through proper time management; Products/services constantly improved; Vision/mission/goals may be achieved; Competitive advantage over competitors; Regular training improves employee skills; Healthy working relationships; Increased market share/financial stability; Improved business image fewer defects.

subject: Business Studies
question_en: Advise businesses on the role of quality circles as part of continuous improvement to processes and systems.
question_af: Gee besighede raad oor die rol van gehaltesirkels as deel van deurlopende verbetering van prosesse en stelsels.
memo_answer: Role of quality circles: Solve quality problems and implement improvements; Investigate problems and suggest solutions to management; Ensure no duplication of activities; Make suggestions for improving processes; Improve quality through regular reviews; Monitor/reinforce strategies; Increase employee morale/motivation; Contribute to organisation improvement; Reduce costs of redundancy; Create harmony and high performance; Build healthy workplace relationships; Improve employee loyalty; Improve communication at all levels.
